ogTitle: East Nabawa vs East Nannup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in East Nabawa and East Nannup.
heading: East Nabawa vs East Nannup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between East Nabawa, Western Australia and East Nannup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: East Nabawa has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to East Nannup (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is East Nabawa or East Nannup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: East Nabawa has a flood risk score of 57/100, while East Nannup scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: East Nabawa scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. East Nannup scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in East Nabawa or East Nannup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. East Nabawa has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
